hints.molis: 'molis' is a noun in the genitive case
hints.Romanam: 'Romanam' is an adjective — it doesn't appear in this phrase

hints.Present: Present of 'esse' would be 'est' — 'erat' has the imperfect stem
hints.Perfect: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuit' — 'erat' uses the imperfect stem
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect of 'esse' would be 'fuerat' — 'erat' is simpler

hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are the principal parts of 'amare' (to love), a 1st conjugation verb
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'ferre' (to carry), an irregular verb
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the principal parts of 'ire' (to go)

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'moles' is 'moles' — 'molis' with -is ending is genitive

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns typically end in -a — 'moles' has a genitive in -is, typical of 3rd
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us or -um — 'moles' with genitive 'molis' is 3rd
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es with genitive -ei (e.g. 'res, rei') — 'molis' is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative feminine singular would be 'tanta' — 'tantae' agrees with 'molis' in genitive
hints.Dative: 'Tantae' could be dative singular, but it agrees with 'molis' which is genitive

translation: So great a task it was
